title: Research: LangGraph current external practice vs MAP's graph/runner.py and agent_loop.py usage
description: Operator asked (hcom broadcast, TASK-144 sibling) to research whether MAP's LangGraph orchestration is aligned with current external practice, since agent training knowledge can be stale. Use MAP_System/templates/research/ end-to-end (first real exercise of the Research System per DEC-027's trigger condition: a genuine external/current-practice technology question) to produce a Research Brief through Research Summary on installed langgraph==1.2.7 vs graph/runner.py (StateGraph/Command/interrupt usage) and agent_loop.py (claim/heartbeat/submit loop), and hand findings to codex-lab-veto (TASK-144, structural assessment/cleanup coordinator) as an input packet rather than editing MAP core files directly.
acceptance_criteria: Research Brief, Source Map, Source Evaluation, Claim Evidence Matrix, Assumption Register, and Research Summary all exist under artifacts/research/ using the templates
Research Summary gives a plain answer on whether MAP's LangGraph usage is current, stale, or missing recommended patterns, with confidence and sourcing
Findings handed to codex-lab-veto/TASK-144 as an input packet, not applied directly to graph/runner.py or agent_loop.py by this task
